studies.title: FML-OG-CAMPAIGN
studies.tag: Best CTR · Lowest CPL · Creative isolation test
studies.objective:
> Validate a new creative-hook framework at a sub-₹15K budget **before any scaling**.

- Validate a single hook × format pairing in isolation (one concept per ad set, no algorithmic bias).
- Establish a CPL ceiling the rest of the campaign portfolio can be benchmarked against.
- De-risk a creative hypothesis that would later be scaled into ₹50L+ monthly spend.
strategy: **One campaign, one ad set, one creative.** Budget = ₹10,766. Zero audience / format variance.
**Placement:** Feed-only, mobile-only — to remove the feed-vs-reels confound that was muddying earlier tests.
**Audience:** Broad (no interests, no behaviours) — to test the creative alone, not the targeting.
**Optimization event:** Leads (instant form).
**Frequency cap:** 2/day per user.
**Decision rule (pre-registered):** CTR ≥ 5% AND CPL ≤ ₹100 → scale to next round. Otherwise kill.
learnings: **Isolated creative testing is non-negotiable.** Without the single-variable setup, this CTR would have been attributed to the audience, not the creative — and the next scale-up would have mystifyingly under-delivered.
**₹10K of validated learning saves ₹5L of failed scale.** The decision rule paid for itself 460×.
**Broad-audience testing under-reports true CTR.** Once this creative was layered with a 1% LAL seed in subsequent campaigns, CTR *rose* — confirming the creative carried the test, not the audience.
**What did NOT work:** The same hook in a static-image format hit 0.8% CTR. The hook's energy only survived in 9:16 video with sound on.
contribution: Wrote the creative brief (hook concept, format, voiceover direction).
Briefed the UGC creator, directed the shoot (via Fiverr async review).
Set up the isolation test in Ads Manager.
Ran the 54-day test solo with a weekly performance check-in.
Folding the winner into scale campaigns (FML ABO MASTER, FML-CBO series).

studies.title: FML ABO MASTER CAMPAIGN
studies.objective:
> Build the **always-on lead-generation backbone** for the master account. Single ABO campaign → 4 ad sets segmented by business-stage signal. Replace a fragmented set of 12+ campaigns running in parallel.

- Consolidate 12+ fragmented lead-gen campaigns into one ABO.
- Hold blended CPL ≤ ₹180 across the consolidated spend.
- Maintain ≥10K leads/month at steady state.
- Improve operational efficiency (1 campaign vs 12+).
strategy: **Campaign structure:** Single ABO, 4 ad sets segmented by business-stage signal (Cold / Warm / Engaged / Retarget).
**Creative pool:** 12 creatives (3 hooks × 4 formats), refreshed monthly. Hooks are FML-OG-winner derivatives.
**Optimization event:** Leads (instant form) with a 24h post-submit qualification step.
**Daily-budget step:** 20% per week once ROAS guard-rail is green.
**Kill threshold:** Any ad set whose 7-day rolling CPL exceeds the campaign median +20% gets paused.
learnings: **ABO beats CBO for lead-gen at this scale.** When creative differentiation is the variable you want to test, ABO gives you cleaner read-throughs than CBO.
**One campaign ≠ fewer learnings.** The 4 ad sets effectively behaved like 4 parallel A/B tests (audience segment × hook).
**The kill threshold is the multiplier.** Pausing the worst performer every Friday freed up ~15% of budget for the winners.
**What did NOT work:** Reels-only creative refresh in Q3 2025 showed 30% lower CTR than the feed-9:16 mix — reverted after 3 weeks.
contribution: Designed the ABO structure and the kill-threshold rule.
Owned the creative refresh cadence (monthly, 12 variants).
Daily monitoring (15 mins), weekly ad-set review (45 mins), monthly creative retrospective (3 hrs).
Reported CPL and volume to the client weekly; flagged any deviation > 15% within 24h.

studies.title: MSXSK Sales Campaign 9.2.26
studies.tag: Largest-spend purchase campaign · DTC · CBO scale
studies.objective:
> Scale the **flagship purchase-optimised campaign** into a 9-figure monthly revenue contributor while keeping blended ROAS above 4×. 4 ad sets split by funnel temperature, 8 creatives per ad set in a hook × format matrix.

- Reach ₹25L+ in monthly spend at a CPP ≤ ₹1,500.
- Hold blended ROAS ≥ 4× (after iOS attribution loss).
- Build the playbook other SSA products could clone.
strategy: **CBO with Advantage+ campaign budget** — let Meta allocate across 4 ad sets by real-time ROAS.
**Funnel-temperature ad sets:** Cold / Warm / Hot / Retarget, each with its own creative brief.
**Creative test:** Hook × Format matrix (8 concepts × 3 formats = 24 variants, isolation-tested at ₹5K each before scale).
**LAL seeding:** 1% / 2% / 3% LALs, refreshed every 14 days.
**Daily-budget step:** 20% per week. Hard pause if 7-day ROAS < 2.5×.
**Attribution:** Pixel + CAPI server-side for iOS recovery.

budget.Value: ₹29,13,371 *(₹29.13L)* — the largest single-campaign spend in the portfolio

learnings: **Testimonial-led video won 71% of head-to-head creative tests.** Founder-talking-head and UGC-with-product demo were the runners-up. Static-image product shots finished last.
**iOS attribution recovery is non-optional.** Adding CAPI restored ~22% of lost purchase events. The CPP dropped from ₹1,615 (pixel-only) to ₹1,266 (CAPI-on).
**20% step rule preserved ROAS.** Going from ₹4K/day to ₹8K/day in three 20% steps kept ROAS in the 4.0–4.5× band. A 100% jump the same week dropped ROAS to 2.8× for 6 days.
**What did NOT work:** Broad-audience lookalikes (no seed) delivered 0.3% CTR — re-introducing interest-stacked audiences for the cold ad set restored CTR to 1.1%.
contribution: Designed the 4-ad-set CBO structure and the creative-test matrix.
Set up CAPI server-side integration with the dev team.
Owned the LAL refresh cadence (14-day).
Reported ROAS and CPP weekly to the founder + the SSA ops team.
Designed the playbook that was cloned to other SSA products (MSXSK Sales Campaign 11/08/25, etc.).

studies.title: RETARGETING-CAMPAIGN [22-12-25]
studies.objective:
> Stand up an **always-on retargeting layer** across 3 mutually-exclusive cohorts with frequency caps and a 30/72 budget reallocation rule. Convert warm traffic at lower cost than cold prospecting.

- Reduce blended CPL by ~30% on retargeted audiences vs cold.
- Hold CTR ≥ 5% (warm traffic signal).
- No frequency-cap violations (max 2/day per user).
strategy: **3 ad sets, mutually exclusive:** Site-visitors (30d) / IG-FB engagers (60d) / Video-75% viewers (30d).
**Frequency caps:** 2/day per user, 7/week per user.
**Budget rule:** 30/72 — 30% of retargeting budget goes to the highest-ROAS cohort, 72-hour reallocation window if a cohort's CTR drops >25% below its 7-day median.
**Creative:** Best-performing cold creative repurposed for warm traffic, plus retargeting-specific LPVs (testimonials, urgency-led hooks).
**Exclusion:** Purchasers (180d).
learnings: **Mutually-exclusive cohorts are essential.** Without the exclusion rule, the 3 cohorts cannibalized each other and the same user was seeing all 3 ad sets. After exclusions, ROAS lifted ~18%.
**Video-75% viewers convert at 2× the rate of site-visitors** — the highest-intent signal in the funnel. This insight rewrote the retargeting priorities across the entire portfolio.
**What did NOT work:** Dynamic Product Ads (DPA) for a service business. We don't sell SKUs; the DPA ads rendered blank creatives for non-product LPs. Killed after 2 weeks.
contribution: Designed the 3-cohort mutual-exclusion structure.
Set the 30/72 budget reallocation rule and the 2/day frequency cap.
Built the retargeting-specific creative pool (testimonials, urgency).
Quarterly retrospective on cohort performance vs cold prospecting.

studies.title: GN - CA - Scale - CBO - oSales
studies.tag: USD scale / Purchase · #1 revenue contributor in USD portfolio
studies.objective: > Scale a winning purchase-optimised campaign from a $2K/day test to a sustained scale. 20% daily-budget step discipline, ROAS guard-rails, LAL refresh cadence.
strategy: **CBO with Advantage+ campaign budget.**
**Audience:** 1-3% LALs (purchaser-seeded), refreshed every 14 days.
**Creative test:** 6 hook variants, 3 formats (UGC / founder-head / motion-graphic) — 18 total creatives.
**Daily-budget step:** 20% per week, hard pause if 7-day ROAS < 2.5×.
**Attribution:** 7-day click + 1-day view window.
learnings: **LAL refresh cadence matters more than creative cadence.** Refreshing LALs every 14 days preserved CTR; refreshing every 60 days let it decay ~18%.
**UGC outperformed founder-talking-head 3:1** in this category (DTC subscription webinar funnel).
**What did NOT work:** Lookalikes seeded from 'video viewers' (vs purchasers) under-converted by ~40%. Always seed LALs from the bottom of the funnel.
contribution: Designed the LAL refresh cadence (14 days).
Owned the 6-hook × 3-format test matrix.
Built the ROAS guard-rail dashboard.
Quarterly retrospective on USD portfolio performance.

studies.title: 2024-ToF-UTIL2-D497-Campaign-Raj
studies.objective: > Drive a high volume of low-cost leads in a utility-style funnel. 4 ad sets segmented by life-stage signal — a hypothesis tested against interest-only.
strategy: **Hypothesis:** Life-stage signals (e.g., 'new parent', 'recently moved') convert better than interest stacks for utility products.
**Test design:** 4 ad sets × 2 audience types (life-stage vs interest) = 8 cells. Equal budget per cell for the first 14 days.
**Optimization event:** Leads (instant form).
**Decision rule:** Best CPL wins; second-best becomes the scale campaign; bottom 6 cells are killed.
learnings: **Life-stage beats interest for utility products.** The 3 winning life-stage ad sets averaged $0.71 CPL; the 4 interest-only ad sets averaged $2.40 CPL.
**Always seed scale campaigns with the winner, not the average.** The single best ad set in this test hit $0.36 CPL — it became the seed for the next quarter's USD scale campaign.
**What did NOT work:** Combining life-stage + interest in the same ad set cannibalized both — keep them isolated.
contribution: Formed the life-stage hypothesis.
Designed the 8-cell test matrix with a pre-registered decision rule.
Ran the test, harvested the winners, scaled.
Wrote the internal doc that became the 'how to test audiences' playbook for the rest of the RAJ-POLANKI portfolio.
**Pre-registered decision rules** on every test (CTR / CPL / ROAS thresholds, kill + scale rules).
**Single-variable isolation tests** for every winning creative and every audience hypothesis.
**20% daily-budget step rule** for any scaling motion.
**Hard kill thresholds** with weekly enforcement.
**Spend-weighted portfolio reporting** instead of row-count averages.
